episode_overview.episode_title: The Mole With a Living Radar
episode_overview.logline: A star-nosed mole uses its biological radar to hunt in complete darkness, revealing one of nature's fastest sensory systems.

hook.hook_line: This tiny mammal hunts faster than the human eye can follow.
hook.curiosity_gap: How does an almost-blind creature catch prey in total darkness?
hook.payoff_setup: The answer lies in the strangest nose on Earth — a living biological radar.

summary.short_summary: The star-nosed mole uses its bizarre tentacled nose as a high-speed touch organ to hunt in complete darkness.
summary.full_summary: A short cinematic wildlife documentary episode about how the star-nosed mole uses its strange nose as a biological radar system to hunt in complete darkness. Featuring macro cinematography, underground tunnel sequences, and extreme close-ups of the mole's unique sensory tentacles.

notes: Strong hook with immediate curiosity gap
Visual descriptions are vivid and production-ready
Consider adding more tension in mid-section pacing

vo_script.full_script:
Scene 1 — 5s
Beneath the wet soil of North America, a creature searches in total darkness.

Scene 2 — 6s
Its nose is not just strange. It is one of the fastest touch organs in the animal kingdom.

lines.voiceover: Beneath the wet soil of North America, a creature searches in total darkness.
Its nose is not just strange. It is one of the fastest touch organs in the animal kingdom.

storyboard.storyboard_copy_prompt:
Create a clean cinematic storyboard sheet for The Mole With a Living Radar, vertical wildlife documentary style, consistent subject, consistent visual style.

Panel 1:
BBC Earth style macro wildlife documentary shot, wet underground tunnel, star-nosed mole moving through dark soil, natural textures, cinematic lighting, no humans
Camera: Macro close-up, shallow depth of field, slow push-in feeling

Panel 2:
Extreme macro shot of star-nosed mole nose tentacles, wet pink sensory rays, dark natural background, high-detail wildlife documentary style
Camera: Extreme macro detail shot, locked camera, intense focus on the nose

Design rules:
- consistent animal appearance
- consistent habitat
- no humans
- no text overlays unless required
- clean panel borders
- professional documentary storyboard layout

panels.subject_action: Star-nosed mole moving through dark soil tunnel
panels.environment: Wet underground tunnel, dark earth textures, roots visible
panels.camera_style: Macro close-up, shallow depth of field, slow push-in feeling
panels.lighting: Cinematic natural lighting, soft rim light from tunnel opening
panels.composition: Low angle tracking shot following the mole from behind
panels.consistency_notes: Maintain wet soil color palette, mole fur texture consistent

panels.visual_beat: Reveal of the star-nosed mole's unique sensory organ
panels.subject_action: Nose tentacles twitching rapidly, scanning tunnel floor
panels.environment: Dark underground tunnel, close dirt walls, moisture visible
panels.camera_style: Extreme macro detail shot, locked camera, intense focus on the nose
panels.lighting: Dramatic documentary lighting highlighting tentacle texture
panels.composition: Extreme close-up centered on nose, shallow depth blurs background
panels.consistency_notes: Same mole as panel 1, same tunnel environment, continuous sequence

image_video_prompts.image_prompt: BBC Earth style macro wildlife documentary shot, wet underground tunnel, star-nosed mole moving through dark soil, natural textures, cinematic lighting, no humans
image_video_prompts.video_prompt: 5-second macro wildlife documentary shot, star-nosed mole moving through a damp underground tunnel, subtle handheld camera feel, shallow depth of field, no humans
image_video_prompts.negative_prompt: Daylight, surface, humans, cartoon style, text overlay, artificial lighting

image_video_prompts.image_prompt: Extreme macro shot of star-nosed mole nose tentacles, wet pink sensory rays, dark natural background, high-detail wildlife documentary style
image_video_prompts.video_prompt: 6-second extreme macro wildlife shot, sensory rays twitching rapidly as the mole scans the tunnel floor, cinematic natural lighting, no humans
image_video_prompts.negative_prompt: Daylight, surface, humans, cartoon style, text overlay, artificial lighting

thumbnail_prompt.prompt: Cinematic macro thumbnail of a star-nosed mole emerging from dark wet soil, strange nose clearly visible, dramatic documentary lighting, high detail, no text
thumbnail_prompt.negative_prompt: Text overlay, cartoon style, human, daylight, low resolution, blurry

social_export.caption: This mole hunts with a living radar hidden beneath the soil.
title_options: The Mole With a Living Radar
The Blind Mole That Sees Everything
social_export.description: Discover how the star-nosed mole uses its bizarre tentacled nose as a biological radar to hunt faster than any other mammal. BBC Earth-style macro wildlife cinematography.
